docs.title: go-code — Claude Code in Go
docs.text: go-code is a Go implementation of Anthropic's Claude Code (Cline) agent system. It provides a command-line interface for interacting with Large Language Models using the Claude API, featuring a full agent loop with tool execution, permission management, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) support.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
go-code implements the core functionality of Claude Code in pure Go, offering:

  • Full Agent Loop: State management, message history, and tool execution
  • 6 Built-in Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, and Bash commands
  • Permission System: User approval for dangerous operations
  • MCP Support: Extensible tool ecosystem via Model Context Protocol
  • Streaming: Real-time token-by-token response streaming

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Installation Guide
  • Quick Start
  • Configuration
  • Architecture Overview
  • Agent Loop
  • Built-in Tools
  • Python Harness

docs.title: Why Go?
docs.text:
This implementation leverages Go's strengths:

  • Performance: Fast execution and low memory footprint
  • Concurrency: Built-in goroutines for parallel tool execution
  • Cross-Platform: Single binary deployment across all major platforms
  • Standard Library: Rich built-in packages for HTTP, JSON, and file operations

docs.title: Status
docs.text: This is an educational implementation demonstrating how Claude Code works under the hood. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by Anthropic.

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT License - see LICENSE for details.

docs.title: Agent Loop
docs.text: The agent loop is the core execution engine that orchestrates the interaction between the model, tools, and user.

docs.title: Message History
docs.text:
The agent maintains a list of messages that includes: - User messages (prompts) - Assistant messages (model responses) - Tool result messages (tool outputs)

type Message struct {
    Role    string    // "user", "assistant", "tool"
    Content string    // Text content
    ToolID  string    // Tool call ID (for tool results)
}

docs.title: Tool Selection
docs.text: When the model responds with tool calls, the agent: 1. Parses the tool call from the model's response 2. Validates the tool exists in the registry 3. Checks permissions for the tool operation 4. Executes the tool with the provided arguments 5. Captures the result and adds it to history

docs.title: Continuation Criteria
docs.text:
The loop continues when: - The model requests tool execution - The response is incomplete (streaming)

The loop terminates when: - The model provides a final text response (no tool calls) - An error occurs - The maximum iterations are reached (to prevent infinite loops)

docs.title: Tool Schema
docs.text:
The model is provided with a schema describing all available tools:

{
  "name": "Read",
  "description": "Read the contents of a file",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "file_path": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Path to the file to read"
      }
    },
    "required": ["file_path"]
  }
}

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The agent loop handles several error conditions:

  • API Errors: Retry with exponential backoff
  • Permission Denied: Skip tool execution, inform model
  • Tool Execution Failed: Return error to model, allow recovery
  • Timeout: Return timeout error to model

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Agent loop behavior can be configured:

# Maximum tool calls per conversation (prevents infinite loops)
max_iterations: 100

# Timeout for tool execution (seconds)
tool_timeout: 30

# Whether to show tool execution progress
verbose: true

docs.title: Related Concepts
docs.text:
• Built-in Tools - Available tool implementations
  • Permission System - Permission handling
  • API Client - Anthropic API communication

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: go-code is organized into several key components that work together to implement the Claude Code agent system.

docs.title: CLI/Repl
docs.text: The command-line interface provides the user-facing entry point. It handles: - Parsing command-line arguments - Managing the interactive REPL session - Streaming user input to the agent

docs.title: Agent Loop
docs.text: The core orchestrator that manages the agent's execution cycle. It: - Maintains conversation history - Decides which tools to call based on model responses - Handles tool execution and result processing - Manages the loop until the task is complete

docs.title: API Client
docs.text: Handles communication with the Anthropic API: - Sends messages with tool definitions - Receives streaming responses - Parses tool call requests from the model - Manages authentication and rate limiting

docs.title: Tool Registry
docs.text: Central registry for all available tools: - Manages built-in tool registration - Handles MCP tool discovery - Provides tool schema to the model - Routes tool calls to appropriate implementations

docs.title: Built-in Tools
docs.text: Six core tools that enable file system and command execution: - Read: Read file contents - Write: Create or overwrite files - Edit: Make targeted modifications - Glob: Find files by pattern - Grep: Search file contents - Bash: Execute shell commands

docs.title: Permission System
docs.text: Ensures user control over dangerous operations: - Intercepts potentially harmful tool calls - Prompts for user approval - Manages auto-approval rules - Logs permission decisions

docs.title: Config Loader
docs.text: Loads and manages configuration: - Reads config files (YAML) - Processes environment variables - Provides configuration to all components - Handles default values

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
internal/
├── agent/          # Agent loop implementation
│   ├── loop.go     # Main loop logic
│   └── history.go  # Message history management
├── api/            # Anthropic API client
│   ├── client.go   # HTTP client
│   ├── stream.go   # Streaming handling
│   └── types.go    # API types
├── config/         # Configuration
│   ├── loader.go   # Config file loading
│   └── types.go    # Config types
├── permission/     # Permission system
│   ├── policy.go   # Permission policies
│   ├── rules.go    # Permission rules
│   └── prompter.go # User prompts
└── tool/           # Tool system
    ├── registry.go # Tool registry
    ├── tool.go     # Tool interface
    ├── builtin/    # Built-in tools
    │   ├── read.go
    │   ├── write.go
    │   ├── edit.go
    │   ├── glob.go
    │   ├── grep.go
    │   └── bash.go
    └── mcp/        # MCP integration
        ├── client.go
        └── transport.go

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
1. User enters a prompt in the CLI/Repl
  2. Agent Loop receives the prompt and adds it to history
  3. Agent Loop sends the full message history to the API Client
  4. API Client sends request to Anthropic API and receives streaming response
  5. If the response contains tool calls, the Agent Loop:
  6. Routes each tool call to the Tool Registry
  7. Checks permissions via the Permission System
  8. Executes the tool and captures the result
  9. Adds the tool result to the message history
  10. Loop continues until the model provides a final response
  11. Final response is displayed to the user

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• Agent Loop - Detailed agent loop explanation
  • Built-in Tools - Tool implementations
  • Python Harness - Test infrastructure

docs.title: Built-in Tools
docs.text: go-code includes six built-in tools that provide the core file system and command execution capabilities.

docs.title: Tool Reference
docs.text: Tool Description Risk Level Read Read file contents Low Write Create or overwrite files Medium Edit Make targeted code edits Medium Glob Find files by pattern Low Grep Search file contents Low Bash Execute shell commands High

docs.title: Read
docs.text:
Reads the contents of a file from the file system.

Schema:

{
  "name": "Read",
  "description": "Read the contents of a file from the filesystem. Use this when you need to examine the contents of a file.",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "file_path": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Path to the file to read"
      }
    },
    "required": ["file_path"]
  }
}

Example:

Tool: Read
Arguments: {"file_path": "src/main.go"}

docs.title: Write
docs.text:
Creates a new file or overwrites an existing file.

Schema:

{
  "name": "Write",
  "description": "Create a new file or overwrite an existing file with new content.",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "file_path": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Path to the file to write"
      },
      "content": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Content to write to the file"
      }
    },
    "required": ["file_path", "content"]
  }
}

Note: Requires user approval unless auto-approve is enabled.

docs.title: Edit
docs.text:
Makes targeted modifications to an existing file. Uses a line-based approach.

Schema:

{
  "name": "Edit",
  "description": "Make a targeted edit to an existing file. Replaces specific lines with new content.",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "file_path": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Path to the file to edit"
      },
      "old_string": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The exact text to replace (must match file content)"
      },
      "new_string": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The replacement text"
      }
    },
    "required": ["file_path", "old_string", "new_string"]
  }
}

Note: Requires user approval unless auto-approve is enabled.

docs.title: Glob
docs.text:
Finds files matching a glob pattern.

Schema:

{
  "name": "Glob",
  "description": "Find files matching a glob pattern.",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "pattern": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Glob pattern (e.g., *.go, **/*.ts)"
      }
    },
    "required": ["pattern"]
  }
}

Example patterns: - *.go - All Go files in current directory - **/*.js - All JavaScript files recursively - src/**/*.ts - TypeScript files in src/

docs.title: Grep
docs.text:
Searches for text within files.

Schema:

{
  "name": "Grep",
  "description": "Search for text patterns in files.",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "pattern": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Regular expression to search for"
      },
      "path": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Path to search in (file or directory)"
      }
    },
    "required": ["pattern", "path"]
  }
}

docs.title: Bash
docs.text:
Executes shell commands.

Schema:

{
  "name": "Bash",
  "description": "Execute a shell command.",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "command": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Shell command to execute"
      }
    },
    "required": ["command"]
  }
}

Note: High risk - always requires user approval.

docs.title: MCP Integration
docs.text: In addition to built-in tools, go-code supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for extending capabilities.

docs.title: Configuring MCP Servers
docs.text:
In config.yaml:

mcp_servers:
  filesystem:
    command: "npx"
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path"]
  github:
    command: "python"
    args: ["-m", "mcp.server.github", "--token", "your-token"]

docs.title: MCP Tool Discovery
docs.text: When MCP servers are configured, the agent: 1. Connects to each MCP server on startup 2. Requests the list of available tools 3. Registers discovered tools in the tool registry 4. Routes MCP tool calls through the MCP client

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: go-code can be configured via config files or environment variables.

docs.title: Config File Location
docs.text:
• Linux/macOS: ~/.config/go-code/config.yaml
  • Windows: %APPDATA%\go-code\config.yaml

docs.title: Config File Format
docs.text:
# API Configuration
api_key: "sk-ant-your-api-key-here"

# Model settings
model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
max_tokens: 4096

# Permission settings
auto_approve_read: true
auto_approve_write: false
auto_approve_bash: false

# MCP servers
mcp_servers:
  filesystem:
    command: "npx"
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/dir"]
  github:
    command: "python"
    args: ["-m", "mcp.server.github", "--token", "your-token"]

# Logging
log_level: "info"
log_file: "go-code.log"

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Required ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Your Anthropic API key Yes ANTHROPIC_MODEL Model to use No (default: claude-sonnet-4-20250514) ANTHROPIC_MAX_TOKENS Max response tokens No (default: 4096) GO_CODE_LOG_LEVEL Log level (debug, info, warn, error) No (default: info) GO_CODE_CONFIG Path to config file No

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
Configuration is loaded in this order (later overrides earlier):

  1. Default values
  2. Config file
  3. Environment variables
  4. Command-line flags

docs.title: Command-Line Flags
docs.text:
--help      Show help message
--version   Show version
--model     Specify model (overrides config)
--config    Specify config file path

docs.title: Model Options
docs.text:
Available models (subject to API availability):

  • claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (default)
  • claude-opus-4-20250514
  • claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022

docs.title: MCP Configuration
docs.text:
Model Context Protocol servers can be configured in the config file:

mcp_servers:
  server_name:
    command: "path/to/executable"
    args: ["arg1", "arg2"]
    env:
      KEY: "value"

See MCP Integration for details.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide covers how to install go-code from source or pre-built binaries.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.23 or later
  • Python 3.x (optional, only needed for the test harness)

docs.title: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/user/go-code.git
cd go-code

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
make build

This creates the binary at bin/go-code.

docs.title: Install to PATH
docs.text:
go install ./cmd/go-code

This installs the binary to $GOPATH/bin (typically ~/go/bin).

docs.title: Pre-built Binaries
docs.text:
Download the appropriate binary for your platform from the releases page:

Platform Architecture Filename Linux amd64 go-code-linux-amd64 macOS amd64 go-code-darwin-amd64 macOS arm64 go-code-darwin-arm64 
# Example: Download and install Linux amd64
curl -L -o go-code https://github.com/user/go-code/releases/latest/download/go-code-linux-amd64
chmod +x go-code
sudo mv go-code /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
# Check the binary exists
ls -la bin/go-code

# Run with --help
./bin/go-code --help

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start - Run your first command
  • Configuration - Set up API keys and preferences

docs.title: 上线指南
docs.text: 本文档指导你如何将 go-code 项目部署到 GitHub 并上线官网。

docs.title: 前置条件
docs.text:
• Git 已安装
  • Go 1.23+ 已安装
  • GitHub 账号

docs.title: 步骤 1：运行一键准备脚本
docs.text:
chmod +x launch.sh
./launch.sh

脚本会自动完成： - 清理非项目目录（owncode-analysis/, claw-code-parity/, test/） - 生成 go.sum - 构建二进制文件 - 运行所有测试 - 初始化 Git 仓库

docs.title: 步骤 2：创建 GitHub 仓库
docs.text:
1. 打开 https://github.com/new
  2. 填写以下信息：
  3. Repository name: go-code
  4. Description: Claude Code in Go — AI-powered coding assistant
  5. Visibility: Public（开源项目）
  6. ⚠️ 不要勾选 "Add a README file"（我们已有 README.md）
  7. ⚠️ 不要勾选 "Add .gitignore"（我们已有 .gitignore）
  8. ⚠️ 不要勾选 "Choose a license"（我们已有 LICENSE）
  9. 点击 Create repository

docs.title: 步骤 3：添加远程并推送
docs.text:
# 替换 YOUR_USERNAME 为你的 GitHub 用户名
git remote add origin git@github.com:YOUR_USERNAME/go-code.git

# 添加所有文件
git add .

# 首次提交
git commit -m "feat: initial release — Claude Code clone in Go

- Agent Loop with tool_use support
- 6 built-in tools (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep)
- Permission system (ReadOnly/WorkspaceWrite/DangerFullAccess)
- MCP integration (stdio transport)
- Context management (token estimation, auto compaction)
- Python Harness (mock server, parity tests, evaluators)
- MkDocs documentation site
- GitHub Actions CI/CD"

# 重命名分支为 main
git branch -M main

# 推送到 GitHub
git push -u origin main

docs.title: 步骤 4：启用 GitHub Pages
docs.text:
1. 打开你的仓库页面：https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/go-code
  2. 点击 Settings（顶部导航栏）
  3. 左侧菜单点击 Pages
  4. 在 Build and deployment 区域：
  5. Source: 选择 GitHub Actions
  6. 保存后，docs.yml 工作流会自动运行

等待约 1-2 分钟，你的文档网站将上线：

https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/go-code/

docs.title: 步骤 5：创建第一个 Release
docs.text:
# 打标签
git tag v0.1.0
git push origin v0.1.0

然后： 1. 打开仓库页面 → Releases → Draft a new release 2. Tag version: 选择 v0.1.0 3. Release title: v0.1.0 - Initial Release 4. Description: 填写发布说明 5. 点击 Publish release

docs.title: 验证代码仓库
docs.text:
✅ README.md 显示在仓库首页
✅ .gitignore 已生效（bin/ 等目录不在 git 中）
✅ LICENSE 已显示
✅ GitHub Actions 工作流已运行（Actions 标签页查看）

docs.title: 验证文档网站
docs.text:
✅ https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/go-code/ 可以访问
✅ 导航栏有 Home, Guide, Architecture, Harness
✅ 页面有 Material 主题样式

docs.title: 验证 Release
docs.text:
✅ Releases 页面有 v0.1.0
✅ 用户可以下载源码

docs.title: Q: GitHub Actions 运行失败？
docs.text: 检查 Actions 标签页的日志。常见原因： - Go 版本不匹配（需要 1.23+） - Python 依赖未安装（harness/requirements.txt）

docs.title: Q: 文档网站 404？
docs.text:
• 确认 Settings → Pages → Source 选择了 GitHub Actions
  • 等待 2-3 分钟，部署需要时间
  • 检查 Actions 标签页中 docs.yml 是否成功运行

docs.title: Q: 想自定义域名？
docs.text: 在 Settings → Pages → Custom domain 中添加你的域名，然后在仓库根目录创建 docs/CNAME 文件。

docs.title: 上线后
docs.text:
上线后，以下功能会自动运行：

功能 触发条件 说明 CI 测试 每次 push/PR Go 测试 + Python Harness 测试 多平台构建 每次 push/PR linux/darwin 二进制 文档部署 docs/ 或 mkdocs.yml 变更 自动部署到 GitHub Pages 依赖更新检查 每周 Dependabot 自动检查更新

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide walks through running go-code for the first time.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before running go-code, you need:

  1. An Anthropic API key
  2. The binary built (see Installation)

docs.title: Option 1: Environment Variable
docs.text:
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-your-api-key-here

Add this to your shell profile (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.) for persistence.

docs.title: Option 2: Config File
docs.text:
Create ~/.config/go-code/config.yaml:

api_key: "sk-ant-your-api-key-here"

docs.title: Run in REPL Mode
docs.text:
Start an interactive session:

./bin/go-code

You should see a prompt where you can type requests:

go-code> What is the current directory?

The agent will use tools as needed to answer your question.

docs.title: Run with a Single Prompt
docs.text:
For one-off commands:

./bin/go-code "Create a hello world program in Go"

This will execute the command and exit.

docs.title: Example Session
docs.text:
$ ./bin/go-code
go-code> List the files in the current directory

[Agent thinking...]
[Using tool: Glob with pattern: *]
[Tool result: Found 3 files: main.go, Makefile, README.md]

The current directory contains 3 files:
- main.go
- Makefile
- README.md

docs.title: Permission Prompts
docs.text:
When a tool needs to perform a potentially dangerous operation, go-code will prompt for permission:

go-code> Delete all files in the current directory

⚠️  This will delete 3 files. Approve? (yes/no): no

Type yes to approve or no to deny.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configuration - Customize behavior
  • Architecture Overview - Understand how it works

docs.title: Mock Server Guide
docs.text: The mock server simulates the Anthropic API for testing and development purposes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The mock server: - Accepts the same API requests as the real Anthropic API - Returns realistic responses based on configured scenarios - Eliminates API costs during development - Enables deterministic testing

docs.title: Starting the Mock Server
docs.text:
# From the harness directory
cd harness

# Run the mock server
python -m mock_server

The mock server runs on http://localhost:8080 by default.

docs.title: Configuring go-code to Use the Mock Server
docs.text:
Set the API base URL in your config or environment:

# Environment variable
export ANTHROPIC_API_BASE=http://localhost:8080
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=mock-key

Or in ~/.config/go-code/config.yaml:

api_key: "mock-key"
api_base: "http://localhost:8080"

docs.title: Response Scenarios
docs.text: The mock server can be configured to return different responses:

docs.title: Simple Response
docs.text: Returns a basic text response without tool calls.

docs.title: Tool Call Response
docs.text: Configures the model to request specific tool execution.

docs.title: Error Response
docs.text: Simulates API errors for testing error handling.

docs.title: Custom Responses
docs.text:
To customize mock responses, edit the mock server configuration:

# harness/mock_server/__init__.py

# Define custom response scenarios
RESPONSES = {
    "simple": {
        "type": "text",
        "content": "This is a mock response."
    },
    "tool_call": {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "name": "Read",
        "input": {"file_path": "test.txt"}
    }
}

docs.title: Testing with the Mock Server
docs.text:
1. Start the mock server
  2. Configure go-code to use it
  3. Run your tests or development sessions
# Terminal 1: Start mock server
cd harness && python -m mock_server

# Terminal 2: Run go-code
cd go-code
export ANTHROPIC_API_BASE=http://localhost:8080
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=mock-key
./bin/go-code "Read test.txt"

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
The mock server: - Does not implement the full Anthropic API - Has simplified tool execution (reads actual files but doesn't run commands) - Cannot replicate complex model behaviors

Use for basic testing and development only. Always test against the real API before release.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Harness Overview - Back to harness documentation
  • Configuration - Configure go-code

docs.title: Python Harness Overview
docs.text: The Python harness is an optional component that provides testing and development utilities for go-code.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The harness serves several purposes:

  1. Mock Server: Provides a mock Anthropic API server for testing without API costs
  2. Replay Tests: Record and replay agent sessions for debugging
  3. Integration Tests: Test the full agent loop with realistic scenarios

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
The harness requires Python 3.x:

# Navigate to harness directory
cd harness

# Install dependencies (if requirements.txt exists)
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
harness/
├── mock_server/       # Mock Anthropic API server
│   └── __init__.py    # Server implementation
├── tests/             # Test suite (if present)
└── pytest.ini         # Pytest configuration (if present)

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Run Python tests only
cd harness && python -m pytest -v

# Or use the Makefile (runs both Go and Python tests)
make test

The Makefile will automatically detect if the harness exists and has tests.

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• Development: Use the mock server during development to avoid API costs
  • CI/CD: Use for continuous integration testing
  • Debugging: Replay tests help reproduce and debug issues

docs.title: When Not to Use
docs.text: The harness is optional. For production use, you only need the Go binary and API key.

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• Mock Server - Detailed mock server guide
  • Architecture Overview - Main architecture
